incidentSummary: Multiple fake Claude AI websites (claude-pro.com, claude-setup.com, claude-docs.com) distributed trojanized installers, fileless infostealers, and PlugX malware. The 'InstallFix' campaign used Google Ads to push malicious download pages. Anthropic also reported 24,000 fake accounts cloning Claude's capabilities. Attacks documented April–May 2026.
urgency: Active malware campaigns are live right now — fake sites distributing infostealers via Google Ads targeting developers

incidentSummary: Gymshark has an entire support page dedicated to warning customers about fake Gymshark websites and social accounts. Cloned stores like gymshark-outlet.com sell counterfeit apparel using stolen product photography. The FraudWear campaign (30,000+ fake stores) specifically targeted Gymshark among 350+ fashion brands. Gymshark updated their fake-site warning page in September 2025.
source: Gymshark Support (Sep 2025), CTM360 FraudWear Report (Feb 2026), Malwarebytes (Mar 2026)
urgency: Ongoing cloned store operations are actively processing payments and damaging brand trust

incidentSummary: Named in Bolster's research as one of 100+ apparel brands targeted by a massive brand impersonation scam campaign using 6,000+ fake sites. FraudWear campaign (30,000+ domains) also targeted Crocs specifically. Fake Crocs stores appear regularly in search ads and social media, selling counterfeit shoes using official product images.
urgency: Part of the FraudWear network — 8,000+ active fake stores at any time selling counterfeit Crocs

incidentSummary: Attackers registered lookalike domains (shopify-login.xyz, shopify-app.xyz) to harvest merchant credentials. Scam ads on Google/YouTube redirect merchants to fake admin login pages. Reddit threads from Feb 2026 show merchants finding exact clones of their stores mirroring changes in real-time. 20,000+ fake shops using Shopify-like templates were documented by Malwarebytes.
urgency: Both Shopify itself AND their merchants are under active attack — 20,000+ fake shops documented

incidentSummary: Australian shoppers lost money to clone Aldi websites selling appliances at fake discount prices. ABC News (Sep 2025) documented victims who found fake Aldi sites through Google Shopping ads. Aldi confirmed they are 'actively pursuing removal' of impersonating sites and working with ACCC and Google. Google had to remove fraudulent sponsored results after ABC inquiry.
source: ABC News Australia (Sep 2025), National Anti-Scam Centre
urgency: Google Shopping ads still show fake Aldi sites — removal is reactive, not proactive

incidentSummary: Over 200 fake Instagram and Twitter accounts using names like @revolut_support and @revolut_official were identified soliciting crypto and personal data. Revolut confirmed the fraudulent accounts were actively scamming users in Q1 2023, and Check Point's Q1 2026 report shows fintech brands remain top phishing targets. 27.1% of callback phishing campaigns impersonated financial services brands Oct 2025–Jan 2026.
source: Revolut fraud alerts (Mar 2023), Financial Times, CybelAngel (May 2026)
urgency: Fintech is the #1 most-targeted sector for phishing — Revolut faces ongoing fake social and phishing campaigns

incidentSummary: Check Point Research identified playstation-stores.com as a phishing website impersonating the official PlayStation Store. The fake site promoted fraudulent discounts and directed users to pay via direct bank transfer — a clear fraud indicator. Check Point's Q1 2026 report documented this as an active campaign.
source: Check Point Research Q1 2026 Brand Phishing Report
urgency: Active fake PlayStation stores are accepting direct bank transfers from victims

incidentSummary: Check Point Research found a fake WhatsApp Web at web.whatsapp.app.hl.cn that replicated the legitimate interface. Victims scanning the QR code unknowingly linked their WhatsApp account to an attacker-controlled session, granting access to private conversations. WhatsApp ranked #8 most-spoofed brand in Q1 2026.
source: Check Point Research Q1 2026 Brand Phishing Report
urgency: QR code hijacking gives attackers real-time access to private messages — immediate threat to 2B+ users

incidentSummary: A malicious website at adobe.donittech.com impersonated Adobe Acrobat, luring users into downloading a trojanized MSI installer that deployed ConnectWise as a Remote Access Trojan. Adobe ranked #7 most-spoofed brand in Q4 2025 (2% of all phishing attacks). The fake download pages are promoted via search engine ads.
source: Check Point Research Q1 2026, Schneider Downs Q4 2025 Report
urgency: Fake Adobe installers are delivering RATs — both brand trust AND user devices are compromised

incidentSummary: Allbirds faced widespread cloning of their website with lookalike domains selling counterfeit wool footwear. CNBC documented the knockoff operations. FraudWear campaign (30,000+ fake fashion stores across 350+ brands) specifically targeted DTC shoe brands like Allbirds. Malwarebytes documented 20,000+ fake shops using cloned templates of legitimate stores including Allbirds.
urgency: FraudWear network has 8,000+ active fake stores at any time — Allbirds remains a persistent target
